docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Vexy Lines for Mac & Windows | Download | Buy | Batch GUI | CLI/MCP | API | .lines format

docs.title: vexy-lines-apy
docs.text:
Python bindings to the Vexy Lines MCP API and style engine.

Connect to the Vexy Lines app over TCP, drive it programmatically, and transfer artistic styles between images — all from Python.

  • On Github
  • On PyPI

docs.title: Two entry points
docs.text:
MCPClient -- a context-managed TCP client that speaks JSON-RPC 2.0 to the Vexy Lines embedded server. Open documents, manipulate layers, tweak fill parameters, render, export. The app auto-launches if it isn't running.

Style engine -- extract a fill style from a .lines file, apply it to any source image, or blend two styles at any mix ratio. No GUI interaction needed.

docs.title: Quick start: MCP client
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient

with MCPClient() as vl:
    vl.open_document("photo.lines")
    info = vl.get_document_info()
    print(f"{info.width_mm:.0f} x {info.height_mm:.0f} mm @ {info.resolution} dpi")

    tree = vl.get_layer_tree()    # LayerNode tree
    vl.render()                    # render + wait for completion
    vl.export_svg("output.svg")

docs.title: Quick start: style transfer
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient, extract_style, apply_style

style = extract_style("reference.lines")

with MCPClient() as vl:
    svg = apply_style(vl, style, "photo.jpg", dpi=72)

with open("result.svg", "w") as f:
    f.write(svg)

docs.title: Quick start: style interpolation
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import extract_style, interpolate_style

a = extract_style("painterly.lines")
b = extract_style("technical.lines")
mid = interpolate_style(a, b, t=0.5)   # halfway between both

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Installation -- install options and optional extras
  • API Reference -- every class, method, and function
  • Style Engine -- how extraction, application, and interpolation work
  • MCP Protocol -- the 25 tools in 5 groups
  • Examples -- real-world usage patterns

docs.title: MCPClient
docs.text:
Context-managed TCP client for the Vexy Lines MCP server.

from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient

with MCPClient(host="127.0.0.1", port=47384, timeout=30.0, auto_launch=True) as vl:
    info = vl.get_document_info()

Constructor args:

Parameter Type Default Description host str "127.0.0.1" Server address port int 47384 Server port timeout float 30.0 Socket timeout in seconds auto_launch bool True Launch the app on connection failure

docs.title: new_document(width, height, dpi, source_image) -> NewDocumentResult
docs.text: Create a new document. Width and height are optional when a source image is provided.

docs.title: open_document(path) -> str
docs.text: Open a .lines file. Returns server status.

docs.title: save_document(path=None) -> str
docs.text: Save the document. Pass a path for Save As.

docs.title: export_document(path, dpi=None, format=None) -> str
docs.text: Export to file. Format: "svg", "pdf", "png", "jpg", "eps".

docs.title: get_document_info() -> DocumentInfo
docs.text: Returns document metadata: dimensions, DPI, units, unsaved changes flag.

docs.title: get_layer_tree() -> LayerNode
docs.text: Returns the full document tree as a recursive LayerNode.

docs.title: add_group(parent_id=None, caption=None, source_image_path=None) -> dict
docs.text: Add a group. Returns dict with "id" of the created group.

docs.title: add_layer(group_id) -> dict
docs.text: Add a layer to a group. Returns dict with "id".

docs.title: add_fill(layer_id, fill_type, color=None, params=None) -> dict
docs.text: Add a fill to a layer. fill_type is e.g. "linear", "circular".

docs.title: delete_object(object_id) -> str
docs.text: Delete any object by ID.

docs.title: get_fill_params(fill_id) -> dict
docs.text: Get all parameters of a fill as a dict.

docs.title: set_fill_params(fill_id, **params) -> str
docs.text:
Set fill parameters by keyword:

vl.set_fill_params(42, color="#ff0000", interval=3.0, angle=45)

docs.title: set_source_image(image_path, group_id=None) -> str
docs.text: Set the source image for a group.

docs.title: set_caption(object_id, caption) -> str
docs.text: Rename an object.

docs.title: set_visible(object_id, visible=True) -> str
docs.text: Toggle visibility.

docs.title: set_layer_mask(layer_id, paths, mode="create") -> str
docs.text: Set an SVG vector mask. Mode: "create", "add", "subtract".

docs.title: get_layer_mask(layer_id) -> dict
docs.text: Get mask data for a layer.

docs.title: transform_layer(layer_id, translate_x=0, translate_y=0, rotate_deg=0, scale_x=1, scale_y=1) -> str
docs.text: Apply a 2D transform to a layer.

docs.title: set_layer_warp(layer_id, top_left, top_right, bottom_right, bottom_left) -> str
docs.text: Set perspective warp corners. Each corner is [x, y].

docs.title: render(timeout=120.0) -> bool
docs.text: Render all layers and wait for completion. Combines render_all() + wait_for_render().

docs.title: render_all() -> str
docs.text: Trigger a render without waiting.

docs.title: wait_for_render(timeout=120.0, poll_interval=0.5) -> bool
docs.text: Poll until rendering finishes.

docs.title: get_render_status() -> RenderStatus
docs.text: Check whether the document is currently rendering.

docs.title: undo() -> str / redo() -> str
docs.text: Undo or redo the last action.

docs.title: get_selection() -> dict | str
docs.text: Get the currently selected objects.

docs.title: select_object(object_id) -> str
docs.text: Select an object by ID.

docs.title: Export shortcuts
docs.text: Method Returns export_svg(path, dpi=None) Resolved Path export_pdf(path, dpi=None) Resolved Path export_png(path, dpi=None) Resolved Path export_jpeg(path, dpi=None) Resolved Path export_eps(path, dpi=None) Resolved Path svg() SVG content as str svg_parsed() svglab.Svg object (requires [svg] extra)

docs.title: call_tool(name, arguments=None) -> dict | str
docs.text: Call any MCP tool by name. Returns parsed JSON dict or raw string.

docs.title: MCPError
docs.text:
Raised when the MCP server returns an error or communication fails.

from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient, MCPError

try:
    with MCPClient(auto_launch=False) as vl:
        vl.get_document_info()
except MCPError as e:
    print(f"MCP failed: {e.message}")

docs.title: extract_style(path) -> Style
docs.text: Parse a .lines file and return a Style containing the full group/layer/fill tree and document properties. Does not need the app.

docs.title: apply_style(client, style, source_image, dpi=72, relative=False) -> str
docs.text:
Apply a style to a source image via MCP. Creates a new document, replicates the style tree, renders, and exports SVG.

When relative=True, spatial fill parameters (interval, thickness, base_width, dispersion) are scaled by the geometric mean of width/height ratios between the source style's document and the target. Keeps styles looking consistent across different image sizes.

docs.title: interpolate_style(a, b, t) -> Style
docs.text: Blend two compatible styles. t=0 returns style A, t=1 returns style B, t=0.5 is the midpoint. Numeric fill parameters and hex colours interpolate linearly.

docs.title: styles_compatible(a, b) -> bool
docs.text: Check whether two styles have matching tree structures (same groups, layers, fills, fill types). Required for interpolation.

docs.title: Style
docs.text: Field Type Description groups list[GroupInfo \| LayerInfo] The fill tree props DocumentProps Document dimensions and stroke limits source_path str \| None Path of the source .lines file

docs.title: DocumentInfo
docs.text: Field Type Description width_mm float Document width in mm height_mm float Document height in mm resolution float DPI units str Measurement units (e.g. "mm") has_changes bool Unsaved changes flag

docs.title: LayerNode
docs.text:
Recursive tree node from get_layer_tree().

Field Type Description id int Object ID for MCP calls type str "document", "group", "layer", or "fill" caption str Display name visible bool Visibility state fill_type str \| None Fill algorithm (only when type == "fill") children list[LayerNode] Child nodes

docs.title: NewDocumentResult
docs.text: Field Type Description status str Status string width float Width in pixels height float Height in pixels dpi float Resolution root_id int Root node object ID

docs.title: RenderStatus
docs.text: Field Type Description rendering bool Whether the document is currently rendering

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: See CHANGELOG.md for the full release history.

docs.title: Open a document and inspect it
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient

with MCPClient() as vl:
    vl.open_document("artwork.lines")
    info = vl.get_document_info()
    print(f"{info.width_mm:.0f} x {info.height_mm:.0f} mm")
    print(f"Resolution: {info.resolution} dpi")
    print(f"Unsaved changes: {info.has_changes}")

docs.title: Walk the layer tree
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient

with MCPClient() as vl:
    vl.open_document("artwork.lines")
    root = vl.get_layer_tree()

    def print_tree(node, depth=0):
        indent = "  " * depth
        label = f"{node.type}: {node.caption}"
        if node.fill_type:
            label += f" [{node.fill_type}]"
        if not node.visible:
            label += " [hidden]"
        print(f"{indent}{label}")
        for child in node.children:
            print_tree(child, depth + 1)

    print_tree(root)

docs.title: Change fill colours and re-export
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient

with MCPClient() as vl:
    vl.open_document("artwork.lines")
    root = vl.get_layer_tree()

    # Find all fill nodes and set them to blue
    def set_all_fills_blue(node):
        if node.type == "fill":
            vl.set_fill_params(node.id, color="#2563eb")
        for child in node.children:
            set_all_fills_blue(child)

    set_all_fills_blue(root)
    vl.render()
    vl.export_png("blue_version.png", dpi=150)

docs.title: Create a document from scratch
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient

with MCPClient() as vl:
    doc = vl.new_document(source_image="photo.jpg", dpi=150)

    # Add a group, layer, and fill
    group = vl.add_group(parent_id=doc.root_id, caption="My Group")
    layer = vl.add_layer(group_id=group["id"])
    fill = vl.add_fill(
        layer_id=layer["id"],
        fill_type="linear",
        color="#333333",
        params={"interval": 2.0, "angle": 45},
    )

    vl.render()
    vl.export_svg("from_scratch.svg")

docs.title: Style transfer: one style, many images
docs.text:
from pathlib import Path
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient, extract_style, apply_style

style = extract_style("artistic.lines")
photos = sorted(Path("./photos").glob("*.jpg"))
output = Path("./output")
output.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

with MCPClient() as vl:
    for photo in photos:
        svg = apply_style(vl, style, photo, dpi=72)
        (output / f"{photo.stem}.svg").write_text(svg)
        print(f"Done: {photo.name}")

docs.title: Style interpolation for animation
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient, extract_style, interpolate_style, apply_style

start = extract_style("soft.lines")
end = extract_style("bold.lines")

frames = [f"frame_{i:04d}.jpg" for i in range(60)]

with MCPClient() as vl:
    for i, frame in enumerate(frames):
        t = i / max(len(frames) - 1, 1)
        blended = interpolate_style(start, end, t)
        svg = apply_style(vl, blended, frame, dpi=72)
        with open(f"output/frame_{i:04d}.svg", "w") as f:
            f.write(svg)

docs.title: Export to multiple formats
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient

with MCPClient() as vl:
    vl.open_document("artwork.lines")
    vl.render()

    vl.export_svg("output.svg")
    vl.export_pdf("output.pdf")
    vl.export_png("output.png", dpi=300)
    vl.export_jpeg("output.jpg", dpi=150)
    vl.export_eps("output.eps")

docs.title: Get SVG as a string
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient

with MCPClient() as vl:
    vl.open_document("artwork.lines")
    vl.render()

    svg_text = vl.svg()
    print(f"SVG length: {len(svg_text)} chars")

    # Use with svglab (install separately: pip install svglab)
    svg_obj = vl.svg_parsed()

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient, MCPError

try:
    with MCPClient(auto_launch=False, timeout=5.0) as vl:
        vl.get_document_info()
except MCPError as e:
    print(f"Could not connect: {e.message}")

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11 or newer
  • The Vexy Lines desktop app (macOS or Windows) for all MCP operations

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text:
pip install vexy-lines-apy

Or with uv:

uv add vexy-lines-apy

docs.title: SVG manipulation
docs.text:
pip install "vexy-lines-apy[svg]"

Adds the svglab package, enabling client.svg_parsed() which returns a full SVG DOM object you can traverse, edit, and render.

docs.title: Runtime dependencies
docs.text: Package Why vexy-lines-py .lines file parser and types loguru Structured debug logging typing-extensions Backported type hints for Python 3.11

docs.title: Verify the install
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient, extract_style
print("vexy-lines-apy is ready")

docs.title: The Vexy Lines app
docs.text:
The MCP client connects to a TCP server embedded in the Vexy Lines desktop app at localhost:47384. By default, MCPClient auto-launches the app if it isn't running.

To disable auto-launch:

with MCPClient(auto_launch=False) as vl:
    ...

The style extraction functions (extract_style, interpolate_style, styles_compatible) work offline -- they parse .lines files directly and don't need the app. Only apply_style and MCPClient methods require a running app.

docs.title: Development install
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/vexyart/vexy-lines.git
cd vexy-lines/vexy-lines-apy
uv venv --python 3.12
uv pip install -e ".[dev]"

Run tests:

uvx hatch test

docs.title: MCP Protocol
docs.text: The Vexy Lines macOS/Windows app embeds an MCP server: a JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint on localhost:47384 over raw TCP with newline-delimited messages.

docs.title: Connection
docs.text:
The server listens on 127.0.0.1:47384 by default. Connect with a TCP socket and perform the MCP handshake before calling tools.

MCPClient handles all of this automatically:

from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient

with MCPClient() as vl:
    # handshake done, ready to call tools
    info = vl.get_document_info()

docs.title: Handshake
docs.text:
1. Client sends initialize with protocol version "2024-11-05" and client info
  2. Server responds with its capabilities and matching protocol version
  3. Client sends notifications/initialized notification
  4. Connection is ready for tool calls

docs.title: Wire format
docs.text:
Each message is a single line of JSON followed by \n:

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"get_document_info"}}

Responses follow the same format:

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"{\"width_mm\":210,...}"}]}}

Tool results are wrapped in content[0].text which contains either JSON or plain text.

docs.title: Document (5 tools)
docs.text: Tool Description Args new_document Create a new document width, height, dpi, source_image open_document Open a .lines file path save_document Save current document path (optional, for Save As) export_document Export to file path, format, dpi get_document_info Get metadata (none)

docs.title: Structure (5 tools)
docs.text: Tool Description Args get_layer_tree Full document tree (none) add_group Add a group parent_id, caption, source_image_path add_layer Add a layer group_id add_fill Add a fill to a layer layer_id, fill_type, color, params delete_object Delete by ID object_id

docs.title: Fill parameters (2 tools)
docs.text: Tool Description Args get_fill_params Get fill params fill_id set_fill_params Set fill params fill_id, plus any param keys

docs.title: Visual (7 tools)
docs.text: Tool Description Args set_source_image Set group source image image_path, group_id set_caption Rename an object object_id, caption set_visible Toggle visibility object_id, visible set_layer_mask Set SVG vector mask layer_id, paths, mode get_layer_mask Get mask data layer_id transform_layer 2D transform layer_id, translate_x/y, rotate_deg, scale_x/y set_layer_warp Perspective warp layer_id, top_left/right, bottom_left/right

docs.title: Control (6 tools)
docs.text: Tool Description Args render_all Trigger render (none) get_render_status Check if rendering (none) undo Undo last action (none) redo Redo last action (none) get_selection Get selected objects (none) select_object Select by ID object_id

docs.title: Coordinates
docs.text: All coordinates are in pixels at the document's DPI. Origin is top-left.

docs.title: Export formats
docs.text: The export_document tool supports: svg, pdf, png, jpg, eps.

docs.title: Error handling
docs.text:
Server errors come back as JSON-RPC error objects:

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"error":{"code":-32000,"message":"No document open"}}

MCPClient raises MCPError with the message string.

docs.title: Bridge server
docs.text: For Claude Desktop and Cursor, vexy-lines-mcp bridges stdio to TCP. It reads JSON-RPC from stdin, forwards to the TCP server, and writes responses to stdout. See the CLI docs for setup instructions.

docs.title: Style Engine
docs.text: The style engine extracts, applies, and interpolates fill structures from .lines files. It bridges the parser (vexy-lines-py) and the MCP client into a high-level workflow: take a style from one file, apply it to a different image.

docs.title: Concepts
docs.text:
A Style is a snapshot of the group/layer/fill tree and document properties from a .lines file. It contains everything needed to replicate the artistic look: which fill algorithms, in what order, with what parameters.

Extraction reads a .lines file and captures the tree. This is offline -- no app needed.

Application creates a new document in Vexy Lines via MCP, rebuilds the fill tree with matching parameters, renders, and exports SVG.

Interpolation blends two compatible styles at any ratio. Numeric parameters and hex colours interpolate linearly.

docs.title: Extract a style
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import extract_style

style = extract_style("reference.lines")

# Inspect what's inside
for node in style.groups:
    print(node)

print(f"Source: {style.source_path}")
print(f"Canvas: {style.props.width_mm} x {style.props.height_mm} mm")

The returned Style is a deep copy -- modifying it won't affect the parsed document.

docs.title: Apply a style to an image
docs.text:
from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient, extract_style, apply_style

style = extract_style("reference.lines")

with MCPClient() as vl:
    svg = apply_style(vl, style, "photo.jpg", dpi=72)

with open("result.svg", "w") as f:
    f.write(svg)

What happens behind the scenes:

  1. Creates a new document with the source image
  2. Walks the style tree and creates matching groups, layers, and fills via MCP
  3. Sets all numeric fill parameters on each fill
  4. Renders and waits for completion
  5. Exports and returns the SVG string

docs.title: Relative mode
docs.text:
By default, apply_style uses absolute mode: fill parameters are applied as-is from the source style. If the target image is much larger or smaller than the original, the style may look different.

Relative mode scales spatial parameters (interval, thickness, base_width, dispersion, vert_disp) to match the target document size:

svg = apply_style(vl, style, "large_photo.jpg", dpi=72, relative=True)

The scale factor is the geometric mean of the width and height ratios between source and target dimensions. Non-spatial parameters (angles, brightness thresholds, ratios) stay unchanged.

docs.title: Interpolate two styles
docs.text:
Blend two styles to create smooth transitions. Both styles must be compatible -- same tree structure, same fill types at each position.

from vexy_lines_api import extract_style, interpolate_style, styles_compatible

a = extract_style("painterly.lines")
b = extract_style("technical.lines")

# Check compatibility first
if styles_compatible(a, b):
    mid = interpolate_style(a, b, t=0.5)  # halfway blend

The t parameter controls the mix:

t value Result 0.0 Identical to style A 0.25 75% A, 25% B 0.5 Halfway between A and B 1.0 Identical to style B

docs.title: What interpolates
docs.text:
• Numeric fill params: interval, angle, thickness, smoothness, uplimit, downlimit, multiplier, base_width, dispersion, shear -- all linearly interpolated
  • Colours: hex RGB channels interpolated independently
  • Document props: width, height, thickness/interval ranges interpolated; DPI kept from style A (integer device setting)

docs.title: What doesn't interpolate
docs.text:
• Fill types (must match)
  • Tree structure (must match)
  • Captions, visibility, masks, grid edges (taken from style A)

docs.title: Compatibility check
docs.text:
Two styles are compatible when they share the exact same structure:

  • Same number of top-level nodes
  • Same node types at each position (group vs layer)
  • Within each group, same number and types of children (recursive)
  • Within each layer, same number of fills with matching fill types
from vexy_lines_api import styles_compatible

if not styles_compatible(a, b):
    print("Styles have different structures -- can't interpolate")

If you pass incompatible styles to interpolate_style, it logs a warning and returns a deep copy of style A unchanged.

docs.title: Animation workflow
docs.text:
Interpolation across a sequence of images creates smooth style transitions:

from vexy_lines_api import MCPClient, extract_style, interpolate_style, apply_style

start = extract_style("start.lines")
end = extract_style("end.lines")
images = ["frame_001.jpg", "frame_002.jpg", "frame_003.jpg"]

with MCPClient() as vl:
    for i, img in enumerate(images):
        t = i / max(len(images) - 1, 1)
        style = interpolate_style(start, end, t)
        svg = apply_style(vl, style, img, dpi=72)
        with open(f"output_{i:03d}.svg", "w") as f:
            f.write(svg)

The CLI command vexy-lines style-transfer --style start.lines --end-style end.lines does this automatically.
